title: Isomorphic Strings
description:
Two strings `s` and `t` are **isomorphic** if the characters in `s` can be
replaced to get `t`, with a consistent one-to-one mapping: each character maps
to exactly one other, and no two characters map to the same one. Return whether
`s` and `t` are isomorphic.
constraints: s and t consist of any valid ASCII characters.

solutions.summary: Track the mapping in both directions so it stays one-to-one.

steps.title: Create both mapping tables
steps.explanation: One map records `s → t`, the other `t → s`, so the relationship is consistent both ways.

steps.title: Check each character pair
steps.explanation: For aligned characters `a` and `b`, any existing mapping must agree; a conflict means not isomorphic.
